title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE COMPOSTELA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL â SAN MIGUEL ANNEX IN BARANGAY SAN MIGUEL, MUNICIPALITY OF COMPOSTELA, PROVINCE OF COMPOSTELA VALLEY FROM THE COMPOSTELA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS SAN MIGUEL NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 5948

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10049, March 19, 2010 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE COMPOSTELA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL â SAN MIGUEL ANNEX IN BARANGAY SAN MIGUEL, MUNICIPALITY OF COMPOSTELA, PROVINCE OF COMPOSTELA VALLEY FROM THE COMPOSTELA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS SAN MIGUEL NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Separation and Conversion into a National High School.

â The Compostela National High School â San Miguel Annex in Barangay San Miguel, Municipality of Compostela, Province of Compostela Valley is hereby separated from the Compostela National High School and converted into an independent national high school to be known as San Miguel National High School.

SEC. 2.

Transfer of Assets and Liabilities.

â All personnel, assets, liabilities and records of the Compostela National High School â San Miguel Annex are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the San Miguel National High School.

SEC. 3.

Appropriations.

â The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the Department's program the operationalization of the San Miguel National High School, the initial funding of which shall be charged against the current year's appropriations of the Compostela National High School â San Miguel Annex. Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the school shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation.

â The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations that may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity.

â This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette

.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JUAN PONCE ENRILE

(Sgd.) PROSPERO C. NOGRALES

President of Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on March 5, 2009 and December 16, 2009, respectively.

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary of Senate

Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: MAR 19 2010

(Sgd.) GLORIA MACAPAGAL - ARROYO

President of the Philippines
